questions.question_text: Does your hospital have a smoke free policy that prohibits smoking anywhere on the hospital grounds?
Does your hospital have a designated smoking area?
If the answer to question 1b is 'yes', to what extent are smoking restrictions enforced in areas outside the designated smoking areas?
If the answer to question 1b is 'no', to what extent are smoking restrictions enforced throughout hospital grounds?

answer_options: Yes external and selected internal sites
questions.question_text: Does your hospital have a vape friendly policy that permits vaping on the hospital grounds?
Does your hospital have designated vaping areas?
Does your trust utilise a standardised clerking proforma which has a dedicated space to document smoking history for newly admitted (emergency and elective) patients? Tick all that apply
Does your trust have a hospital-based smoking cessation service on the premises?
Other, please specify for question 5a:
If yes to 5a, is this provided by the trust or an external provider?
Does your trust have access to a community-based smoking cessation service?
Other, please specify for question 5c:
Is there a formal referral pathway that allows all healthcare professionals within your hospital to refer patients to a hospital-based smoking cessation service within your Trust?
Is there a formal referral pathway that allows all healthcare professionals within your hospital to refer patients to a community smoking cessation service?
Is smoking cessation in your hospital supported by a consultant or nurse consultant?
If yes, how many hours per week are allocated in this person's job plan to smoking cessation?
Does your hospital have a dedicated Hospital Smoking Cessation Practitioner (HSCP)?
If yes, how many hours per week is an HSCP/are HSCPs employed?
If yes, how many HSCPs are employed?
Please confirm which post your HSPC holds. If more than one HSPC, please provide information for most senior.

Is pharmacotherapy for tobacco addiction available in your hospital?
If the answer to 9a is yes, which? Tick all that apply
If the answer to 9a is yes, does your hospital have a prescribing guideline / protocol to support clinicians when prescribing pharmacotherapy for tobacco addiction?
Does the support provided to patients include an offer of a vaping kit?
Is the HSCP able to prescribe, supply or recommend pharmacotherapy that can be given to inpatients on the day of the consultation? Tick all that apply
Is the HSCP able to prescribe, supply or recommend pharmacotherapy that can be given to outpatients on the day of the consultation? Tick all that apply

Do inpatients have access to an HSCP during their stay?
Do outpatients have access to an HSCP during their visit?
Does your hospital-based smoking cessation service have the following? Tick all that apply

answer_options: An initial consultation lasting up to 40 minutes
Weekly follow-up appoints of 10-20 minutes for at least 4 weeks in the first month after the stop date
A validated method for confirming a patient has stopped smoking 4 weeks after the quit date
Phone call contact at 3 and 6 months
Self-reported quitters offered a final appointment at 12 months
questions.question_text: Does your hospital-based smoking cessation service, offer the following? Tick all that apply

answer_options: Yes – via hospital smoking cessation service
Yes – via community smoking cessation service
questions.question_text: For smokers that are discharged from your hospital that wish to stop smoking, is there a system in place that allows them to be followed up by a smoking cessation service (hospital or community) for ongoing support?
Are frontline hospital staff offered regular smoking cessation training?
If yes, which staff are offered smoking cessation training? Tick all that apply:
